scenarios.title: Python Agent Frameworks
scenarios.when_to_read: Tasks about building, debugging, or migrating CrewAI agents, tasks, crews, flows, tools, memory, RAG, LLM providers, or multimodal workflows.
scenarios.role: Provides self-contained CrewAI routing, verified APIs, workflow references, and safe diagnostics for the CrewAI Python framework and CLI.
scenarios.read_when: CrewAI, crewai, crewai_tools, Agent, Task, Crew, Flow, crew.jsonc, crewai CLI, MCP, CrewAI memory, CrewAI tools, CrewAI tracing, multimodal input_files.
scenarios.best_for: Designing crews and flows, validating JSONC projects, configuring provider/model settings, choosing tools/MCP/RAG/file workflows, troubleshooting CLI/runtime errors, and avoiding unsafe LLM or credential-bound execution.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is unrelated to CrewAI or asks about another agent framework without CrewAI migration or integration needs.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `crewai` when the user names CrewAI or uses CrewAI-specific APIs, config files, CLI commands, or error messages. Route to sub-skills by workflow rather than source package names.

scenarios.title: Repository Development
scenarios.when_to_read: Tasks about contributing to the CrewAI monorepo, selecting focused tests, editing docs, or respecting CrewAI repository-specific contributor rules.
scenarios.role: Captures CrewAI monorepo layout, docs snapshot rules, workspace package boundaries, and safe maintainer verification guidance.
scenarios.read_when: CrewAI checkout, AGENTS.md docs rules, docs/edge, docs/v snapshots, Mintlify docs, lib/crewai tests, crewai workspace pyproject, release freeze, docs image rules.
scenarios.best_for: Choosing focused pytest/ruff/mypy/docs checks, editing Edge docs safely, avoiding frozen snapshot and image hazards, and mapping changed paths to native verification candidates.
scenarios.avoid_when: The user is only building an application with installed CrewAI and is not editing the CrewAI repository itself.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Use the repo-development route only for current-checkout contribution tasks; normal CrewAI app usage should start at core-runtime, flows, CLI/projects, tools/MCP, or provider sub-skills.
